title: Andinocleome moritziana
taxonomy: Andinocleome moritziana is a species of flowering plant in the Bignonaceae family. It belongs to the genus Andinocleome, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The plant is a member of the Bignonaceae family, also known as the clockvine family, which consists of about 30 genera and 300 species. Within the Bignonaceae family, Andinocleome moritziana is part of the Andinocleome genus, which contains about 12 species. The exact classification of Andinocleome moritziana is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Lamiales, Family: Bignonaceae, Genus: Andinocleome, Species: A. moritziana.

intro: Andinocleome moritziana, also known as Andinocleome, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cleomaceae. It is a perennial herb with a long history of traditional use in folk medicine, particularly in the Andean region, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Andinocleome moritziana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
